author: Walgreens Patterson Park / Esperanza Health staff
content:
PHARMACY COMMUNICATION / CARE COORDINATION NOTE — 8/22/2026, 9:30 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Contact: Pharmacy callback documented by Esperanza Health care coordinator (Carolina Vasquez, MA, bilingual).

REASON FOR CONTACT:
Pharmacist Mike Tran (Walgreens Patterson Park) called the Esperanza Health line to alert us that patient's HCQ refill could not be filled today. The manufacturer PAP renewal (submitted 6/18/2026 by Ms. Mercado, LCSW) was DENIED on 8/14/2026 because the patient was unable to provide updated proof of household income within the manufacturer's specified window. Without PAP, the cash price is approximately $145 for 60 tablets, which patient/family cannot pay.

Walgreens has held the prescription as 'unfilled / financial hold.' Patient was informed in person when she came in this morning expecting to pick up her refill. Her last filled HCQ was 5/12/2026 (60 days x 2 = ~110 days into the prior PAP supply); patient reports she has been out of HCQ since approximately 8/12/2026 (~10 days). She has continued levothyroxine ($4 generic) without interruption.

PATIENT INTERIM SELF-REPORT (via Carolina, by phone):
- Joint pain is significantly worse over the past 2 weeks. Morning stiffness back to >2 hours. Bilateral wrist and hand pain interfere with her cleaning work. She has had to call out of two shifts in the last week.
- Fatigue is significantly worse — she reports going to bed immediately after coming home from work and 'sleeping through the weekend.'
- New mild rash described as 'pinkish' on her chest and upper back, intermittent over the past 3 weeks; she initially attributed it to sun exposure from sitting outside on lunch break. Did not initially think to call.
- She has noticed her hair shedding has worsened.
- Reports loose pants — has not weighed herself.
- Denies fever, chest pain, dyspnea, oral ulcers, hematuria, foamy urine, eye redness, focal weakness.

INTERVENTIONS TODAY:
1. Carolina (care coordinator) contacted Hopkins rheumatology nursing line to request urgent re-evaluation in light of medication lapse and clinical worsening. Earliest available slot offered: 10/14/2026 (~7 weeks). Patient/mother declined to schedule due to inability to pay the $480 self-pay new-patient or established-patient visit fee (Hopkins financial-aid grant was a one-time award per program rules).
2. Carolina submitted urgent request for emergency HCQ supply through the Hopkins outpatient pharmacy charity program. Decision expected within 1 week. In the interim, no HCQ is available.
3. Carolina booked patient for PCP visit at Esperanza Health on 9/15/2026 (next available with Dr. Williams who has seen her previously). PCP can re-issue a prescription if a lower-cost pharmacy pathway can be identified.
4. Diana Mercado (LCSW) paged for Medicaid appeal hearing follow-up; she will reach out to patient/family this week regarding 8/12/2026 hearing outcome (decision was pending at time of this call).
5. Spanish-language patient education re-shared regarding red-flag symptoms requiring ED evaluation: new rash with fever, chest pain, dyspnea, oral or genital ulcers, dark urine, foamy urine, swelling, vision change, severe abdominal pain, focal weakness.

ASSESSMENT:
Medication continuity has failed. Patient is now off hydroxychloroquine due to non-renewal of patient-assistance program. Clinically she is showing increased joint symptoms, fatigue, and a possible new rash that warrants in-person evaluation. The fragmented care system is now actively driving disease worsening.

PLAN:
- Patient and mother counseled to seek ED evaluation IF she develops any of the red-flag symptoms listed above.
- Esperanza PCP visit on 9/15/2026 to assess clinical status, attempt to bridge HCQ via a different financial pathway, and re-engage with rheumatology if Medicaid appeal succeeds.
- Continue Medicaid appeal follow-up via Ms. Mercado.
- Contact time: 45 minutes (including phone interpretation, family update, and care coordination calls).
attestation:
Documented by: Carolina Vasquez, MA — Esperanza Health Care Coordination
Co-signed by: Dr. Andre Williams, MD (Esperanza Health PCP)
Date: 8/22/2026
